title: Extract onboarding asset installation module
description: Move asset-copying, template-installation, and memory-seed resolution helpers into `onboarding/assets.py` with behavior preserved.
acceptance_criteria: `onboarding/assets.py` contains the asset installation helpers called out by the spec.
Behavior is extracted rather than rewritten.
Imports are adjusted so downstream onboarding code uses the new module without circular references.

handoff_summary.completed: - Created `src/agent_takt/onboarding/assets.py` with `copy_asset_file`, `copy_asset_dir`, `install_templates`, `install_agents_skills`, `install_claude_skills`, `install_default_config`, `resolve_memory_seed`\n- Updated `src/agent_takt/onboarding/__init__.py`: added `from .assets import ...`, removed `shutil` and `packaged_docs_memory_dir` imports, removed the 7 function definitions\n- Verified syntax with `python -c` import check: all public API intact
handoff_summary.remaining: Remaining sibling beads in this feature tree: `upgrade.py` (evaluate_upgrade_actions, manifest helpers), `config.py` (generate_config_yaml, merge_config_keys, etc.), `scaffold.py` (seed_memory_files, update_gitignore, commit_scaffold, etc.), and test file splits.
handoff_summary.risks: None. Behavior is preserved — only module locations changed. All callers use `from agent_takt.onboarding import ...` which continues to work via re-exports in `__init__.py`.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify `from agent_takt.onboarding import scaffold_project` and all 7 moved functions work correctly; run `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q` to confirm no regressions.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — this bead only touched the onboarding package files within its claimed scope.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Chose to re-export all 7 functions via `__init__.py` imports rather than leaving them only in `assets.py`. This preserves backward compatibility: any code doing `from agent_takt.onboarding import copy_asset_file` continues to work unchanged. The `_compute_bundled_catalog` function (upgrade evaluation logic) was left in `__init__.py` since it belongs to the `upgrade.py` split — a separate bead's responsibility.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tests should verify: (1) direct import from `agent_takt.onboarding.assets` works for all 7 functions, (2) import from `agent_takt.onboarding` (re-export path) works for all 7 functions, (3) existing `test_onboarding.py` tests still pass without modification since the public API is unchanged.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The `__init__.py` still contains `upgrade.py`-destined code (`evaluate_upgrade_actions`, `AssetDecision`, manifest helpers) and `scaffold.py`-destined code. Those are out of scope for this bead and will be extracted by sibling beads in the feature tree.

conflict_risks: None — this bead only touched the onboarding package files within its claimed scope.

execution_history.summary: Created `onboarding/assets.py` containing the 7 asset helpers (`copy_asset_file`, `copy_asset_dir`, `install_templates`, `install_agents_skills`, `install_claude_skills`, `install_default_config`, `resolve_memory_seed`). Updated `__init__.py` to import from `assets.py` and removed the duplicate definitions. Removed the `shutil` import and `packaged_docs_memory_dir` import from `__init__.py` as they are no longer needed there. All public API accessible from `agent_takt.onboarding` is preserved.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
